commit.message:
Replace broken apt-repo-update action with native reprepro steps

The apt-repo-update@v1.1 action used a debian:buster Docker base image
whose apt repos are now 404 (buster is EOL), causing every run to fail.

Replace with direct reprepro invocations on the ubuntu-latest runner:
- Install reprepro from the Ubuntu apt mirror
- Import the signing key and cache the passphrase in gpg-agent
  via a dummy sign with --pinentry-mode loopback
- Run 'reprepro includedeb' for each .deb in updates/ across all distros
- Clean up updates/ and commit/push the refreshed apt/ tree

Also adds jammy to the distro loop (was previously missing from the
old workflow's repo_supported_distro list despite being in apt/conf/).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: configurable context compaction threshold + cumulative history context (#315)

* feat: configurable context compaction threshold and cumulative history context

- Add AutoCompact (bool, default true) and CompactThresholdPercent (int, default 75)
  to TuiSettings; Normalize() clamps threshold to 0-100
- Fix premature compaction bug in InteractiveLoop: replace hardcoded 3000-token
  trigger with percentage-of-context-window logic using TuiSettings values;
  falls back to 3000 when model context window is unknown
- Reload TuiSettings fresh each iteration so /config set compact_threshold
  takes effect immediately without restart
- Add CumulativeContextTokens and ContextWindowTokens to TurnRecord for
  point-in-time context snapshot per turn
- Populate CumulativeContextTokens via TokenEstimator.EstimateTokens(History)
  in AgentSession.RecordAssistantTurnAsync after the turn is appended
- Copy new fields in ForkSessionAsync turn clone
- HistoryViewer.RenderDetail now shows 'Context: X,XXX tokens (Y%)' when
  CumulativeContextTokens > 0; gracefully omits % when window is unknown
- Expose compact_auto and compact_threshold in /config get/set/list
- Expand TuiSettingsTests with compact defaults, normalization, round-trip tests
- Expand SessionRecordTests with CumulativeContextTokens/ContextWindowTokens coverage

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address critical code review findings in context compaction

- Fix CRITICAL: CompactThresholdPercent=0 no longer triggers compaction on
  every turn; guard added (AutoCompact && CompactThresholdPercent > 0) in
  InteractiveLoop. Updated doc comment: use compact_auto off to disable.
- Fix HIGH: replace stale _selectedModel.ContextWindowTokens with
  (_session.CurrentModel ?? _selectedModel).ContextWindowTokens so model
  switches mid-session are respected in the compaction threshold.
- Fix HIGH: CompactAsync now derives MaxContextWindowTokens from
  CurrentModel?.ContextWindowTokens (fallback 4000) instead of hardcoded 4000.
  The hardcoded guard floor changes from <= 2000 to <= modelWindow/4 so it
  scales with the actual model window.
- Fix SUGGEST: remove redundant ternary in HistoryViewer; add ~ prefix to
  percentage estimate (CumulativeContextTokens can exceed window as it is
  an approximation).

Co-authored-by: erikshafer <12145838+erikshafer@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: two E2E CI failures — MudSelect click target and Wolverine SignalR injection

Failure 1 (CheckoutPage.cs): SelectAddressByNicknameAsync timed out (30s) because
AddressSelect.Locator('.mud-select-input').ClickAsync() targeted an element that
Playwright's hit-test could not reach in headless Chromium (MudBlazor renders a
transparent overlay on top of .mud-select-input). Reverted to AddressSelect.ClickAsync()
(outer wrapper), which was proven to open the dropdown reliably in the prior CI run.

Failure 2 (CheckoutFlowStepDefinitions.cs + OrderConfirmation.razor):
InvokeMessageAndWaitAsync timed out because OrderStatusChanged and ShipmentStatusChanged
have no local Wolverine handlers — they are SignalR-transport-only messages. Wolverine's
TrackedSession waits for a handler terminal state that never fires. Replaced both calls
with direct IHubContext<StorefrontHub>.SendAsync('ReceiveMessage', ...) injection using a
CloudEvents-shaped payload matching Wolverine's transport format.

Latent bug also fixed: OrderConfirmation.razor's OnSseEvent handler was looking for
'NewStatus' and 'TrackingNumber' (PascalCase) in JsonElement. SignalR serializes via
System.Text.Json camelCase defaults, so properties arrive as 'newStatus'/'trackingNumber'.
This was hidden because InvokeMessageAndWaitAsync always timed out before any message
reached the browser.

* fix: MudSelect Force=true on .mud-select-input, split timeouts to 15s/10s, update docs

Four CI failures all shared the same root cause: SelectAddressByNicknameAsync.
AddressSelect.ClickAsync() (outer wrapper) clicked the bounding-box centre which
landed on the <label> element in headless Chromium — MudBlazor received no open
event, so [role='listbox'] never appeared within 10 seconds.

Fix: Force=true on .mud-select-input bypasses Playwright's hit-test (which was
blocked by MudBlazor's transparent .mud-input-mask overlay) and dispatches the
click directly to the element carrying MudBlazor's @onclick handler.

Also:
- Split MudSelectPopoverTimeoutMs into MudSelectListboxTimeoutMs (15s) and
  MudSelectOptionTimeoutMs (10s) — two distinct wait phases deserve distinct constants
- Updated Pattern 3 and Pitfall 4 in e2e-playwright-testing.md with full
  three-failure-mode history and Force=true rationale

commit.message:
cfl: rewrite icc_tiffdump_fuzzer V3 for coverage breakthrough

Complete rewrite with 2-phase architecture:

Phase 1 — In-memory libtiff:
- Read ALL TIFF tags (planarconfig, photometric, sampleformat, etc.)
- Pixel data via TIFFReadEncodedStrip() + TIFFReadScanline()
- ICC profile extraction + deep exercise (Validate, tag iteration, Describe)
- Multi-directory TIFF support

Phase 2 — CTiffImg file-based:
- Open(), ReadLine(), GetPhoto(), GetIccProfile()
- Exercises contiguous + separated planar paths
- Exercises all 6 photometric type mappings

OOM hardening:
- Profile size guard (256MB cap, 1KB minimum for tag exercise)
- Per-tag size/offset bounds checking vs declared profile size
- MPE amplification guard (tSize * 1024 > profileSize)
- 64-tag iteration cap, 64KB Describe() output cap

Coverage: 7,896 → 9,203 edges (+16.6%) in 2 minutes.

Dictionary: 79 new entries for CTiffImg code paths (planarconfig,
photometric types, sample formats, compression, resolution tags).

Migrate compute from EKS to ECS Fargate

Replace the EKS cluster and Helm-based deployment with ECS Fargate:

Terraform:
- Add ecs.tf: ECS cluster (Container Insights), Fargate task definitions
  for API (port 8080) and frontend (port 80), ECS services, ECS tasks
  security group, CPU-based auto-scaling for both services, CloudWatch
  log groups (/ecs/{env}/api and /ecs/{env}/frontend)
- Add alb.tf: public ALB, HTTP→HTTPS redirect listener, HTTPS listener
  with host-based routing (api_domain → API TG, default → frontend TG)
- Add acm.tf: ACM certificate with DNS validation; auto-provisions
  Route 53 CNAMEs when route53_zone_id is set
- Update iam.tf: replace IRSA + EBS CSI roles with ECS task execution
  role (ECR + SSM) and API task role (S3 + SSM); update CI user policy
  to ECS deploy permissions (RegisterTaskDefinition + UpdateService)
- Update rds.tf / redis.tf: security groups now reference ECS tasks SG
  instead of EKS node group SG
- Update ssm.tf: add thumbnail_bucket_name and redis_connection_string
  SSM parameters so ECS can inject them at task start
- Update vpc.tf: remove EKS-specific subnet discovery tags
- Update variables.tf: replace EKS node vars with ECS Fargate capacity
  vars (cpu, memory, desired_count, max_count per service) + TLS vars
- Update outputs.tf: replace EKS outputs with alb_dns_name, alb_zone_id,
  ecs_cluster_name, acm_validation_records
- Delete eks.tf, k8s-addons.tf (nginx ingress, cert-manager, Prometheus)
- Update environments/*.tfvars accordingly

CI/CD (.github/workflows/deploy.yml):
- Replace Helm upgrade step with ECS task definition registration +
  aws ecs update-service; secrets no longer pass through GitHub Actions
  (ECS agent injects them from SSM at task start)

commit.message:
Fix build system: add dl_find_object stub for musl compatibility

This commit resolves three CRITICAL audit findings:

1. Build system failure for Phase 0 validation (cmd/wain)
2. Inconsistent static linking across demonstration binaries
3. Missing wayland-demo binary

Root Cause:
-----------
GCC 14+ libgcc_eh.a references _dl_find_object (glibc 2.35+), which
musl libc does not provide. This caused link failures when building
with musl-gcc and -static flags.

Solution:
---------
- Created internal/render/dl_find_object_stub.c with weak stub that
  returns -1, triggering libgcc_eh's fallback frame discovery path
- Updated Makefile to compile stub and link it with all CGO binaries
- Simplified pure-Go demo builds (demo, wayland-demo, x11-demo) to
  use CGO_ENABLED=0 for guaranteed static linking
- Added stub cleanup to 'make clean' target

Verification:
-------------
- All binaries now build successfully: wain, demo, wayland-demo,
  x11-demo, widget-demo, gen-atlas
- All binaries are statically linked (verified with 'file' command)
- All Go tests pass (29 packages tested)
- No code complexity regressions (go-stats-generator diff)
- Actually improved: xdg.HandleEvent complexity decreased 60%

Files Modified:
---------------
- Makefile: Added DL_STUB_SRC/OBJ variables, stub build target,
  updated all demo build targets, updated clean target
- AUDIT.md: Marked 3 CRITICAL findings as [x] fixed
- internal/render/dl_find_object_stub.c: New stub implementation

commit.message:
fix: use $(SUFFIX) in version-check binary path for debug builds (#8148)

The version-check step in `build-jaeger` hardcoded the binary name
without `$(SUFFIX)`, so when invoked via
`_build-platform-binaries-debug` (which sets `DEBUG_BINARY=1` →
`SUFFIX=-debug`), the check either failed with "binary not found" or
silently validated the release binary instead of the debug one.

- **`scripts/makefiles/BuildBinaries.mk`**: Replace
`./cmd/jaeger/jaeger-$(GOOS)-$(GOARCH)` with
`./cmd/jaeger/jaeger$(SUFFIX)-$(GOOS)-$(GOARCH)` in both version-check
invocations — no-op for release builds (`SUFFIX` is empty), correct for
debug builds (`SUFFIX=-debug`).
